utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , james , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hey , good to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's good to see you , too . so , i know the nurse told you about dax .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: james is a 57-year-old male with a past medical history significant for congestive heart failure and type 2 diabetes who presents today with back pain .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , james , what happened to your back ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , i was golfing and i hurt my back when i went for my backswing .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and did you feel a pop or a strain immediately or ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i f- felt the pop , and i immediately had to hit the ground . i had to just try and do anything to loosen up my back .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and how long ago did this happen ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: this was saturday morning .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , about four days ago ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , and what have you taken for the pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , i took some tylenol . i took some ibuprofen .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i tried ice . i tried heat , but nothing really worked .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , h- how are you feeling now ? are you still in the same amount of pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , by monday morning , it loosened up a little bit , but it's still pretty sore .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . any other symptoms like leg weakness , pain in one leg , numbing or tingling ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , i actually felt , um ... i had a struggle in my right foot like dropped foot . i had some struggling with my right leg . i felt that for a while , and it got a little bit better this morning but not much .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , so , are you ... how are you doing walking around ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , uh , uh , i'm , i'm not going anywhere fast or doing anything strenuous but i can walk around a little bit .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh- .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not too fast .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . okay . um , and any history with your back in the past ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i actually had surgery about 10 years ago on my lower back .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . now , tell me a little bit about your , your heart failure . you know , i have n't seen you in a while .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how are you doing with your diet ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , been pretty good t- taking my medications , watching my diet , trying to , uh , trying to exercise regularly , too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , you're avoiding the salty foods like we had talked about ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and any weight gain or swelling in your legs recently ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: a little bit of weight gain over the summer but nothing , nothing too radical , nothing more than five pounds .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and any problems laying flat while you go to bed ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . uh , and lastly , what about your diabetes ? how are you doing with , with that diet ? i remember you have somewhat of a sweet tooth .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: jelly beans ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i love jelly beans , yeah , yeah . that's been a struggle , but i'm getting through it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , and you're watching your blood sugars at home ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm . i monitor it regularly . not always, i can forget, , but i'm pretty good about my measuring it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and you are still on your metformin ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . all right . now , i know the nurse did a review of symptoms sheet when you checked in .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i know that you were endorsing the back pain-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . and maybe a little weakness in your right leg . um , any other symptoms ? i know we went through a lot .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , so , i wan na go ahead and move on to a physical exam , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the vital signs . so , here in the office , you know , your vital signs look great . they look completely normal , which , which is really good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay ? so , i'm just gon na check you out , and i'm gon na let you know what i find , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: lean up . okay . all right . so , on your physical exam , everything seems fine .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: on your heart exam , i do appreciate a 2 out of 6 systolic ejection murmur , which we've heard in the past-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . so that's stable .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: on your back exam , you do have some pain to palpation of the lumbar 5 or lumbar spine-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: at the level of l5 .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you have , you know , decreased range of motion with flexion and extension , and , um , you have a positive straight leg raise . uh , for your strength , you do have a 4 out of 5 on your right and 5 out of 5 on your left .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , what does that mean ? what does all that mean ? so , that basically means that , you know , i , i think that you probably , you know , have injured your , your back with a muscle strain , but we're gon na look at some of your results , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the back x-ray . so , in reviewing the results of your back x-ray , this is a normal x-ray of your lumbar spine . there's good boney alignment . i do n't see any abnormality there , which is not surprising based on the history , okay ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the diabetic labs . and this is just ... i just wanted to check your last , uh , diabetic labs that we did on you . uh , it looks like your hemoglobin a1c has been a little high at 8 . i'd like to see that a little bit lower around 7 , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , so , let's just talk a little bit about my assessment and my plan for you . um , so , for your first problem , i think you have an acute lumbar , um , strain .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and i wan na go ahead and prescribe meloxicam 15 milligrams once a day , and i'd like to refer you to physical therapy to kind of strengthen that area . now , if you're still having symptoms , i wan na go ahead and , uh , order an mri-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . just to make sure that you do n't have any disc herniation or anything like that , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's fine .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how does that sound ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no problem .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , order meloxicam 15 milligrams once a day . for your next problem , your type 2 diabetes , i would like to increase your metformin to 1,000 milligrams twice daily-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . and i wan na go ahead and order another hemoglobin a1c in a couple weeks , or , i'm sorry , a couple months .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right ? hey , dragon , order a hemoglobin a1c . and for your congestive heart failure , uh , i think you're doing really well with it . um , you know , i wan na just continue you on your current medications , your lisinopril and your lasix . now , do you need a refill-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: actually , i-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: of the lisinopril ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: actually , i do .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey , dragon , order a refill of lisinopril 20 milligrams once a day . and so , the nurse will come in . she's gon na help you get checked out . i wan na see you again in a couple weeks , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's fine .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , any questions ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not at this point .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey , dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: James Allen is a 57-year-old male with a history of congestive heart failure and type 2 diabetes presenting with back pain.
Back pain
The patient reports back pain starting during a golf backswing.
The doctor asks if the patient felt a pop or strain.
The patient felt a pop and had to lie on the ground to loosen his back.
The doctor asks about the duration of the injury.
The injury occurred on Saturday morning.
The doctor asks what the patient has taken for pain.
The patient took Tylenol and ibuprofen.
The patient used ice and heat without relief.
The doctor asks about the patient's current pain level.
The pain improved slightly by Monday but remains sore.
The doctor asks about leg weakness, pain, numbness, or tingling.
The patient reports right leg weakness and drop foot that improved slightly this morning.
The doctor asks about past back history.
The patient had lower back surgery 10 years ago.
Congestive heart failure
The doctor asks about the patient's diet.
The patient is taking medications, watching his diet, and exercising.
The doctor asks if the patient is avoiding salty foods.
The doctor asks about recent weight gain or leg swelling.
The patient reports less than 5 pounds of weight gain over the summer.
The doctor asks if the patient has difficulty lying flat.
The patient denies difficulty lying flat.
Diabetes
The doctor asks if the patient is monitoring blood sugars at home.
The patient monitors blood sugar regularly but is not always consistent.
The doctor asks if the patient is still taking metformin.
The patient confirms he is still taking metformin.
The patient's vital signs are normal.
Physical exam shows a stable grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur.
Physical exam shows pain to palpation at L5.
Physical exam shows decreased range of motion, positive straight leg raise, and 4/5 right leg strength.
Lumbar spine X-ray is unremarkable with good bony alignment.
Hemoglobin A1c is elevated at 8.
Initiate meloxicam 15 mg once daily.
Refer to physical therapy.
Order MRI if symptoms persist.
Increase metformin to 1000 mg twice daily.
Repeat hemoglobin A1c in a couple of months.
Continue lisinopril 20 mg once daily and Lasix.
The doctor asks if the patient needs a refill of lisinopril.
Refill lisinopril 20 mg once daily.
Follow-up in 2 weeks.

case_description: back pain; congestive heart failure;type 2 diabetes
